title: Find Relief from Jet Lag with These 8 Potent Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for jet lag because it helps the body adjust to new sleep schedules by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and wake up at the right time after traveling.
Valeranone in Panax Ginseng is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate our body's internal clock and improve sleep quality by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
Valerenal in Panax Ginseng is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock and improves sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.
Isovaleric acid is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock by reducing fatigue and promoting a natural sleep-wake cycle.
Valerenol in Panax Ginseng helps with jet lag because it has a regulating effect on the body's internal clock, reducing the stress and fatigue caused by travel across different time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with jet lag. This is because it contains valerenic acid, a chemical that can help regulate the body's internal clock. When we travel across time zones, our body's natural rhythms get out of sync, causing fatigue, insomnia, and other problems. Valeriana officinalis cream works by calming the nervous system and promoting relaxation, which can help the body adjust to the new time zone. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that often accompany jet lag. By using Valeriana officinalis cream, you can promote better sleep and reduce the negative effects of jet lag, making it easier to adjust to your new surroundings. Valeriana officinalis cream is also easy to apply and can be used at any time of day, making it a convenient remedy for jet lag.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Panax ginseng herbal creams that help with jet lag, start by preparing the herbal extract. You'll need dried Panax ginseng roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a solvent like ethanol or glycerin. Combine 1 part of the dried Panax ginseng roots with 2 parts of the carrier oil in a clean glass jar, and let it steep in a cool, dark place for about 2 weeks. This process is called infusion, and it will help the oil absorb the beneficial properties of the ginseng roots. After the infusion, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, and discard the solids. Then, add a few drops of the solvent to the liquid extract, and stir well to dissolve the ginseng compounds. Finally, mix the extract with a base ingredient like shea butter or beeswax, and apply it to your skin as needed to help alleviate jet lag symptoms, such as fatigue and insomnia.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress caused by travel, which can improve sleep quality and reduce fatigue associated with jet lag.

Linalyl acetate is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce stress and fatigue caused by long-distance travel, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
Lavandulol is good for jet lag because it helps the body adjust to new sleep-wake cycles by regulating the body's internal clock and improving the quality of sleep.

Rosmarinic acid is good for jet lag because it can help regulate the body's internal clock and improve sleep quality by increasing the levels of melatonin, a hormone that controls our sleep-wake cycles.
Caryophyllene helps reduce jet lag because it promotes better sleep quality by regulating the body's internal clock and reducing inflammation caused by travel stress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams, or lavender creams, can be helpful in reducing jet lag. When you travel across time zones, your body gets confused about the new sleep-wake cycle. This can cause problems like fatigue, headaches, and difficulty sleeping. Lavender creams contain a natural oil that helps calm your mind and body. This oil, called linalool, has a soothing effect that can reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel. It can also improve sleep quality by relaxing your muscles and promoting a restful night's sleep. By using lavender creams before bedtime, you can help your body adjust to the new time zone and reduce the symptoms of jet lag. Additionally, lavender creams can also help with other issues like insomnia and restlessness, making them a useful tool for travelers.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Panax ginseng herbal cream that helps with jet lag, you will need dried Panax ginseng roots, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a small amount of vitamin E oil. Start by infusing the dried Panax ginseng roots in the carrier oil in a saucepan over low heat for about an hour, then let it cool and strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a bowl. In a separate saucepan, melt the beeswax over low heat, then add the infused Panax ginseng oil, vitamin E oil, and a small amount of water to create a smooth, creamy texture. Continue to heat the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the cream has thickened, then remove it from the heat and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, you can transfer the Panax ginseng herbal cream to a jar and store it in the refrigerator to use as needed. To help with jet lag, apply a small amount of the cream to your skin before bedtime and massage it in gently, as the active compounds in Panax ginseng may help regulate your body's internal clock and promote a restful night's sleep.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids in Panax Ginseng help with jet lag by reducing stress and improving sleep quality, which is essential for recovering from travel fatigue and adjusting to new time zones.

Polyphenols in Panax Ginseng are good for jet lag because they help to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce the negative effects of fatigue and stress caused by traveling across different time zones.
Tannins are good for jet lag because they help to reduce inflammation and improve sleep quality by regulating the body's circadian rhythms, allowing people to adapt more easily to time zone changes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata cream is a natural remedy that can help with jet lag. This is because it contains a chemical called harmine, which affects the brain's internal clock. When you travel across time zones, your body's internal clock gets confused and it's hard to adjust. Passiflora incarnata cream works by helping your brain to adjust to the new time zone. It does this by regulating the production of a hormone called melatonin, which controls sleep and wakefulness. Melatonin levels usually drop in the morning and rise at night, but jet lag disrupts this natural cycle. Passiflora incarnata cream helps to stabilize melatonin levels, allowing you to fall asleep at the right time and wake up feeling refreshed. This natural remedy can also help to reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag. By using Passiflora incarnata cream, you can help your body to adjust to the new time zone more quickly and easily.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Panax ginseng herbal cream that helps with jet lag, first, you need to prepare the herbal extract. Start by drying 100 grams of Panax ginseng roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°C for 24 hours. Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder. Then, mix 10 grams of the powder with 100 milliliters of hot distilled water in a clean glass jar. Let it steep for 2 hours, then strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container. Discard the solids and store the liquid extract in the refrigerator. Next, make the cream by mixing 10 grams of beeswax, 20 grams of shea butter, and 10 grams of coconut oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat until the wax and oils are fully melted. Remove the saucepan from the heat and slowly add 50 milliliters of the Panax ginseng extract to the melted mixture. Stir well until the cream is smooth and consistent. Pour the cream into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely before applying it to the skin. Use this cream on the affected areas, such as the wrists, ankles, or behind the ears, to help alleviate jet lag symptoms like fatigue and insomnia.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Rosmarinic acid is good for jet lag because it helps to adjust the body's internal clock by reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant activity, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as fatigue and disorientation caused by traveling across different time zones.

Luteolin in Panax Ginseng is good for jet lag because it helps regulate the body's internal clock by reducing inflammation, improving sleep quality, and increasing the production of melatonin, a hormone that controls our sleep-wake cycles.
Apigenin is good for jet lag because it helps adjust body clocks by regulating the body's natural sleep-wake cycle and reducing inflammation caused by travel stress.

Quercetin may help alleviate jet lag symptoms because it can regulate body's internal clock by controlling the levels of melatonin, a hormone that helps with sleep-wake cycles, making it easier to adapt to new time zones.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis cream is a natural remedy that can help with jet lag. The main ingredient, Melissa officinalis, is an herb also known as lemon balm. This herb has been used for centuries to calm the mind and body. When traveling across time zones, our body's internal clock can get confused, leading to fatigue, insomnia, and other problems. Melissa officinalis cream can help to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce stress and anxiety. By using Melissa officinalis cream, you can help your body adjust to the new time zone and feel more rested and refreshed. It is often used in aromatherapy and can be applied topically to the skin before bed. The scent of lemon balm can also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Panax ginseng herbal creams that help with jet lag, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Panax ginseng root, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Panax ginseng root with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. Let the liquid cool and then mix it with 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in the double boiler. Heat the mixture until the beeswax and shea butter melt, then remove it from the heat and stir in 1 teaspoon of vitamin E oil. Pour the mixture into a jar and let it cool and solidify before using it as a cream to help your body adjust to a new time zone and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for jet lag because they help to regulate the body's internal clock by reducing fatigue and improving sleep quality, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
Saponins in Panax Ginseng are good for jet lag because they help regulate the body's internal clock and improve the quality of sleep by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.
Flavonoids are good for jet lag because they help to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce fatigue by improving sleep quality and antioxidant defenses.
Fatty acids in Panax Ginseng are good for jet lag because they help the body adjust to new sleep-wake cycles by reducing stress, improving sleep quality, and regulating the body's internal clock.
Phenolic acids are good for jet lag because they help regulate the body's internal clock by reducing stress and improving sleep quality, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa cream is a natural remedy that can help with jet lag. This is because it contains a plant extract that helps to regulate our body's internal clock. When we travel across time zones, our body's internal clock gets confused, leading to problems like fatigue, insomnia, and digestive issues. Avena sativa cream works by reducing stress and anxiety caused by jet lag, and also by regulating our body's cortisol levels. Cortisol is a hormone that helps our body to adjust to new situations, but when it's out of balance, it can cause problems like fatigue and digestive issues. By using Avena sativa cream, we can help to calm our body and mind, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone. This can also help to improve the quality of our sleep, making it easier to wake up feeling refreshed and ready to go.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Panax ginseng herbal cream to help with jet lag, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: Panax ginseng powder, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, vitamin E oil, and a small glass jar. Begin by melting the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or in the microwave in short intervals, stirring between each interval until they're fully incorporated. Next, add the shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until it's smooth. Now, add the Panax ginseng powder and mix well until it's evenly distributed. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before stirring in the vitamin E oil. Pour the mixture into the prepared glass jar and let it cool completely before using. Apply a small amount to your skin, such as on your wrists or behind your ears, to help your body adjust to the new time zone and reduce jet lag symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols in Panax Ginseng are good for jet lag because they contain a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and stress caused by traveling across different time zones, promoting better sleep and overall well-being.
Shogaols are good for jet lag because they help regulate body clocks by increasing the production of melatonin, a hormone that controls sleep and wake cycles.

Curcumenols may help with jet lag because they can reduce stress and fatigue by regulating the body's internal clock and improving sleep quality, which can help people recover faster from long-distance travel.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale cream, also known as ginger cream, can be helpful in managing jet lag. This is because ginger has natural properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, two common problems people experience when traveling across time zones. When you travel, your body gets confused about the new time zone, and this can cause stomach problems and make you feel queasy. Ginger cream can ease these symptoms by reducing inflammation in the stomach and helping to calm the digestive system. Additionally, ginger contains compounds that can help regulate body temperature and improve circulation, which can be beneficial when your body is trying to adjust to a new time zone. By using ginger cream, you can help your body recover from the effects of jet lag and feel more comfortable during your travel.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Panax ginseng herbal cream that helps with jet lag, you will need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, start by infusing dried Panax ginseng roots in a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Let the mixture steep for about 2-3 hours, or overnight, to allow the ginseng's active compounds to extract into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, add a small amount of beeswax and a carrier emollient, such as shea butter or sweet almond oil, to the infused oil, and heat the mixture gently until the wax has melted and the ingredients are fully combined. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool and thicken, then transfer the cream to a clean container and store it in the fridge to help slow down the oxidation process. When you're ready to use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your skin, ideally before bedtime, to help promote a restful night's sleep and alleviate the symptoms of jet lag.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bilobalide is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce fatigue caused by long-distance travel, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
Ginkgolide B is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce inflammation caused by travel, making it easier to recover from long-distance travel.

Flavonoids in Panax Ginseng are good for jet lag because they help to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce oxidative stress caused by long-distance travel, which can disrupt sleep patterns and lead to fatigue.
Quercetin is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce inflammation caused by travel-related stress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba cream can be good for jet lag because it helps to improve blood flow to the skin. When we travel across different time zones, our body's internal clock gets confused. This can cause fatigue, headaches, and skin problems. Ginkgo biloba cream contains a special extract that helps to increase blood flow, which brings more oxygen and nutrients to the skin. This can help to reduce puffiness, dark circles, and other signs of fatigue. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe irritated skin and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles. Additionally, ginkgo biloba cream may help to improve cognitive function, which can aid in adjusting to a new time zone. It can also help to reduce stress and anxiety that often come with traveling.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Panax ginseng herbal cream that helps with jet lag, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Panax ginseng root, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and essential oils like lavender or peppermint. First, dry the Panax ginseng root in a low-temperature oven (150-200°F) for 2-3 hours to preserve its medicinal properties. Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the mixture is fully melted, add the ground Panax ginseng powder and stir well to combine. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before pouring it into a clean, sterilized container. Allow the mixture to cool and harden completely before adding a few drops of your chosen essential oil and stirring well. Once the cream is fully set, it's ready to use as a topical remedy to help alleviate jet lag symptoms, such as fatigue, insomnia, and stress.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for jet lag

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Ginsenosides in Panax Ginseng is good for jet lag because it helps to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce fatigue by improving the balance of hormones that control sleep and wake cycles.

Saponins are good for jet lag because they help regulate body rhythms and improve sleep quality by reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant activity, allowing the body to adapt quickly to new time zones.

Polyphenols in Panax Ginseng are good for jet lag because they help to regulate the body's internal clock, improve sleep quality, and increase alertness during the day, making it easier to adjust to new time zones.

Triterpenoids are good for jet lag because they help regulate the body's internal clock and reduce fatigue caused by crossing different time zones, allowing for a smoother transition and quicker recovery.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng creams are believed to be helpful in reducing the effects of jet lag. This is because ginseng is thought to improve blood flow and energy levels, which can be low after long flights. When you travel across time zones, your body's internal clock gets confused. Ginseng creams may help to regulate your body's clock and make you feel more alert and awake. They also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce fatigue and discomfort caused by travel. Additionally, ginseng is often used to boost the immune system, which can help prevent illnesses that come with traveling, such as colds and flu. By using Panax ginseng creams, you may be able to adapt to the new time zone more quickly and feel more refreshed and revitalized.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Panax ginseng herbal creams that help with jet lag, you will need dried Panax ginseng roots and some basic herbal skincare ingredients. First, dry the Panax ginseng roots completely, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator, to prevent any mold or bacterial growth. Next, grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Then, combine the ginseng powder with a carrier oil, such as coconut or jojoba oil, in a ratio of about 1 part ginseng powder to 5 parts carrier oil. You can also add other herbal ingredients like aloe vera or chamomile to enhance the cream's soothing properties. Finally, mix the ginseng-infused oil with a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to create a rich and luxurious cream that can be applied directly to the skin to help alleviate jet lag symptoms like fatigue and inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal creams for jet lag

intro_desc: Jet lag is a common problem that can disrupt your sleep and daily routine when traveling across different time zones. This can lead to feelings of fatigue, irritability, and difficulty concentrating. To alleviate these symptoms, using herbal creams that contain soothing herbs like Valeriana officinalis and Lavandula angustifolia can be beneficial. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is a natural relaxant that can help calm your mind and body, promoting a good night's sleep. It contains a compound called valerenic acid, which has a sedative effect on the nervous system, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender oil, is known for its calming and soothing properties, which can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by jet lag. When applied topically as a cream, these herbs can be absorbed into the skin, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.  It's essential to address jet lag because it can significantly impact your daily life. When you're feeling fatigued and irritable, it can be challenging to perform daily tasks, maintain relationships, and stay productive at work. Additionally, chronic sleep deprivation can lead to more severe health problems, such as cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and obesity. By using herbal creams with Valeriana officinalis and Lavandula angustifolia, you can help regulate your sleep patterns, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve your overall well-being. This can enable you to adapt to new time zones more efficiently, stay focused, and enjoy your travels without the discomfort of jet lag.
supplementary_best: For jet lag remedy, combine Valeriana officinalis and Avena sativa herbal creams. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, helps with sleep issues caused by jet lag. It contains a compound called valerenic acid, which promotes relaxation and calms the nervous system. Avena sativa, or oat straw, is known for its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adjust to new environments and time zones. It contains a compound called avenanthramides, which improve circulation and reduce stress. By combining these two herbs, you get a cream that not only promotes sleep but also helps the body adapt to the new time zone.
supplementary_avoid: If you have jet lag, some herbal creams to avoid are those containing Peppermint (Mentha piperita). This is because Peppermint can stimulate your body and make it harder to fall asleep. Jet lag is already causing trouble with your sleep, so you need to stay calm and relaxed. Peppermint can also cause stomach upset, which can make jet lag symptoms worse. Another herb to avoid is Wild Yam (Dioscorea villosa), which can cause drowsiness and make you feel more tired. However, this might not be a bad thing for jet lag, but it's not a solution to the problem either. The problem is that Wild Yam can interact with other medications, such as blood thinners, and make them less effective. Another herb to avoid is Devil's Claw (Harpagophytum procumbens), which can cause stomach upset and make your jet lag symptoms worse. Devil's Claw can also interact with other medications and make them less effective. Finally, another herb to avoid is Ginger (Asarum canadense), which can cause stomach upset and make your jet lag symptoms worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: When we travel across time zones, our bodies have trouble adjusting to the new schedule. To help with jet lag, we can use natural remedies like melatonin supplements. Melatonin is a hormone that helps regulate our sleep-wake cycle, and taking it before bedtime can signal to our body that it's time to sleep. This can be especially helpful for people who have trouble falling asleep or staying asleep when they're in a new time zone.  Another natural remedy for jet lag is getting some morning sunlight. When we travel to a new time zone, our bodies are confused about what time it is, and getting some sunlight can help our body understand that it's daytime. This can help regulate our circadian rhythms, which are the internal clocks that control our sleep-wake cycle. By getting some morning sunlight, we can help our body adjust to the new time zone faster.  Exercise can also help with jet lag. When we exercise, our body releases endorphins, which are chemicals that help us feel more alert and awake. Exercise can also help us sleep better at night, which is important when we're trying to adjust to a new time zone. However, it's best to exercise in the morning or early afternoon, when it's still daylight, to help our body stay awake and alert.  Finally, staying hydrated is also important when we're trying to adjust to a new time zone. Dehydration can make it harder for our body to adjust to the new time zone, and it can also make us feel more tired and sluggish. Drinking plenty of water and other fluids can help our body stay hydrated and help us feel more alert and awake.  It's also worth noting that some people find that taking a warm bath or shower before bedtime can help them relax and fall asleep faster. This is because the warm water can help our body relax and calm down, making it easier to fall asleep.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience jet lag often feel tired, dizzy, and have headaches due to a sudden change in time zones. Medicinal herbs can help alleviate these symptoms. For tiredness, Ashwagandha is a herb that can help the body adapt to stress and fatigue. It works by reducing cortisol levels, which can become high due to jet lag. Ashwagandha also improves sleep quality, helping to counteract the disrupted sleep patterns caused by traveling across time zones.  For dizziness and lightheadedness, Ginger is a herb that can help. Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can ease nausea and reduce dizziness. It also helps to regulate blood flow, which can become disrupted due to changes in altitude or air pressure. Ginger can be consumed as tea, added to food, or taken as a supplement.  For headaches, Feverfew is a herb that can help. Feverfew contains a compound called parthenolide, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. It can help to reduce the severity of headaches and ease migraine symptoms. Feverfew can be taken as a supplement or added to tea.  These herbs work by reducing inflammation, regulating blood flow, and improving sleep quality. They can help alleviate symptoms associated with jet lag and promote a faster recovery.
